paper.title_en: Improving <math><mi>α</mi></math>-decay energy and half-life predictions with microscopic energy corrections
paper.abstract_en: A microscopic energy term is introduced into the <math><mi>α</mi></math>-decay energy correlation formula and further extended to half-life predictions, substantially improving the predictive accuracy of both quantities, particularly in regions near nuclear shell closures. For 96 reference-target pairs, the root-mean-square deviation (RMSD) of the predicted <math><mi>α</mi></math>-decay energies decreases from 0.052 to 0.018 MeV upon inclusion of the correction. Building upon the Royer systematics, we develop an improved neighboring-nuclei <math><mi>α</mi></math>-decay half-life law (the NR formula) by incorporating the microscopic energy term. For 170 even-even nuclei, the RMSD of predicted <math><mrow><msub><mo form="prefix">log</mo><mn>10</mn></msub><msub><mi>T</mi><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>/</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></math> values decreases from 0.324 to 0.186, representing a 43% improvement over the original Royer formula.
